guideline_references: eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) — Haemochromatosis / iron overload management
ANZCOR / NHFA-CSANZ Australian Clinical Guidelines for the Diagnosis and Management of Atrial Fibrillation 2018
intentional_test_features: Multi-morbidity systemic-disease trap: a single infiltrative iron-overload disorder presenting as several apparently-separate problems (new atrial fibrillation, RUQ pain/hepatomegaly, recently-diagnosed diabetes, chronic MCP-joint arthralgia, bronze skin, hypogonadism). The test is whether the AI Doctor links the constellation rather than treating each symptom in isolation.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present out-of-hours home-visit clinician who measured vitals, palpated an irregularly-irregular pulse with a pulse deficit and firm hepatomegaly, and ran a 12-lead ECG confirming rapid AF. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, take vitals without a device, run an ECG, or obtain iron studies/LFTs — it is scored on recognising a likely new tachyarrhythmia plus heart-failure risk in a systemically-unwell patient and arranging urgent emergency transfer, NOT on confirming the diagnosis.

Acute-on-chronic tiering: the case is tiered to the ACUTE complication (new sustained rapid irregular arrhythmia with breathlessness and heart-failure signs, with documented risk of haemodynamic collapse / thromboembolic stroke), not to the chronic metabolic label.

No-dose discipline: rate-control and anticoagulation dosing (the hospital answer) and any oral-hypoglycaemic change are prescribing acts requiring ECG/bloods and a clinician — the AI Doctor must NOT mint a dose. Therapeutic phlebotomy is the definitive treatment once diagnosed and is education, not an AI prescription.
Age inferred as adult (~55, male) from hypogonadism/loss of libido, adult child present, and the classic middle-aged-male iron-overload phenotype — flagged; exact age not stated in source.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source note EMD-015.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
